llm_keywords.evidence: |I| ranges from 8.90e-103 A at -0.1 V to 2.3994540300343092e-05 A at -6 V, a span of about 97.43 decades.
As |V| increases from 0.1 V to 1.0 V to 3.0 V to 6.0 V, |I| rises from about 10^-103 A to about 10^-15 A to about 10^-7 A to about 10^-5 A.
Matched |V| values show similar |I| on both sides, for example 0.2 V: 1.744e-54 A vs 1.731e-54 A, 1.0 V: 6.62e-15 A vs 7.83e-15 A, and 6.0 V: 2.399e-5 A vs 2.249e-5 A.

At larger |V| the sequence is not strictly smooth; for example on the positive side |I| changes from 1.856e-5 A at 5.45 V to 1.795e-5 A at 5.50 V to 1.300e-5 A at 5.55 V, then back to 1.482e-5 A at 5.60 V.
All listed currents are negative for negative voltages, such as -2.40e-05 A at -6 V and -6.62e-15 A at -1 V.
All listed currents are positive for positive voltages, such as 6.899103373692976e-103 A at 0.1 V and 2.2494143112878067e-05 A at 6.0 V.
